q: Match List - I with List - II . List - I List - II A. Coefficient of viscosity B. Surface tension C. Pressure D. Surface energy I. [ML $^{-1}$ T $^{-2}$ ] II. [ML $^{2}$ T $^{-2}$ ] III. [ML $^{0}$ T $^{-2}$ ] IV. [ML $^{-1}$ T $^{-1}$ ] Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
explanation: From Newton's law of viscosity, the force is F = \eta A ( \Delta v \Delta x ) , where A is area and \Delta v \Delta x is the velocity gradient. \eta = F A ⋅ ( \Delta v \Delta x ) ⇒ [ \eta ] = $[M^{1}L^{1}T^{1} - 2 ]$ $[L^{2} ]$ ⋅ ( $[L^{1}T^{1} - 1 ]$ $[L^{1} ]$ ) = $[M^{1}L^{1}T^{1} - 2 ]$ $[L^{2} ]$ ⋅ $[T^{1} - 1 ]$ = $[M^{1}L^{1} - $1 ext{ T}$ ^{1} - 1 ]$ A \to IV Surface tension is defined as the force acting per unit length. Force Length T = Force Length $[T^{1} ]$ = $[M^{1}L^{1}T^{1} - 2 ]$ $[L^{1} ]$ = $[M^{1}L^{0} T^{1} - 2 ]$ B \to III Pressure is defined as force acting per unit area. Force Area P = Force Area [ P ] = $[M^{1}L^{1}T^{1} - 2 ]$ $[L^{2} ]$ = $[M^{1}L^{1} - $1 ext{ T}$ ^{1} - 2 ]$ C \to I Surface energy ( U s ) is defined as the work done by surface tension in changing the surface area U s = T ⋅ ( \Delta A ) ⇒ [ U S ] = [ Surface Tension ] ⋅ [ Area ] ⇒ [ U S ] = $[M^{1}L^{0} T^{1} - 2 ]$ ⋅ [ $[M^{0} L^{2} T^{0} ]$ ] = [ $[M^{1}L^{2} T^{1} - 2 ]$ ] D \to II List - I List - II A. Coefficient of viscosity IV. B. Surface tension III. C. Pressure I. D. Surface energy II. Hence, the correct option is (B). List - I List - II A. Coefficient of viscosity IV. $[M^{1}L^{1} - $1 ext{ T}$ ^{1} - 1 ]$ B. Surface tension III. $[M^{1}L^{0} T^{1} - 2 ]$ C. Pressure I. $[M^{1}L^{1} - $1 ext{ T}$ ^{1} - 2 ]$ D. Surface energy II. $[M^{1}L^{2} T^{1} - 2 ]$ Hence, the correct option is (B).
